text.title: test-tdd-red-green-split-agents Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for test-tdd-red-green-split-agents. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: The RED sub-agent skill frontmatter MUST list only `Read` and `Write` (no `Edit`), and Write MUST be path-restricted to `tests/`. The GREEN sub-agent skill frontmatter MUST list only `Read` and `Edit` (no `Write`), and Edit MUST be path-restricted to `src/`. Neither skill may include `Bash` for file moves. Any deviation fails the gate and the cycle aborts.
rule.rationale: Tool-permission discipline is the only mechanism that prevents a single LLM context from "fixing" a failing test by mutating it; trust-based prompts ("please do not edit the test file") fail under sufficiently long sessions.

rule.statement: Between RED and GREEN turns the orchestrator MUST: (1) run the project's test command on the new test, (2) require exit code != 0, (3) abort and surface "first-try-pass" if exit code == 0, (4) pass to GREEN ONLY the failing test diff and the runner's failure stanza — never the original AC, never the test source. After GREEN, the orchestrator re-runs the same command and refuses to commit unless exit code == 0.
rule.rationale: A passing first-run test means either the feature already exists or the test does not exercise the spec; either case is a hard signal to halt rather than continue. Keeping the AC out of GREEN's prompt prevents AC paraphrasing from leaking implementation hints back into the test.
